content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Eduardo Olivo 
City Attorney 
2535 Commerce Way 
Commerce, California 90040 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-110 
Dear Mr. Olivo: 
June 23, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Councilmember Hugo 
Argumedo regarding his duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act 
(the "Act").1 You stated in your letter that the matter was set before the Commerce City Council on 
June 7, 2016. However, on June 1, 2016, you stated that the decision had been continued to a future 
·meeting. 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section 1090. 
QUESTION 
May Councilmember Argumedo participate in decisions regarding the Commerce Retail 
Center Specific Plan despite the fact that his residence is located within 500 feet from the proposed 
location of the proposed project? 
CONCLUSION 
Based on the magnitude of the project that is the subject of the governmental decision in 
relation to that property's current use, the councilmember would have a conflict of interest. 
However, under the "public generally" exception the councilmember may participate in the 
decision. 
FACTS 
The City Council will be considering approval of a Specific Plan that will pennit the 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Go\'emment Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of1he California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-16-110 
Page No. 2 
development of a big box retailer, a bank, and other retail/restaurant uses. Councilmember 
Argumedo's house is located within 500 feet of the proposed project site (approximately 350 feet). 
On May 31, 2016, you clarified that the councilmember's property is north of East Washington 
Street and northwest of South Atlantic Street. His residence is not located on either Washington or 
Atlantic. 
According to the Commerce Retail Center Specific Plan: 
Currell/ use. The entire site has been previously graded associated with the existing projects 
located within the Specific Plan Area and is relatively flat. The site contains existing developed 
commercial and industrial uses. Buildings on the project site total 178,445 square feet. Current uses 
include trucking, warehouse/distribution, manufacturing, equipment rental and repair, retail, and 
medical clinic. Most of the parcels are improved with buildings, surface parking areas, and 
ornamental landscaping and were primarily developed between the 1940s and 1960s. 
The existing project pattern within the Specific Plan area includes an intermixing of obsolete 
commercial buildings with industrial uses in a manner that lacks cohesion between these uses and 
the surrounding community. Accordingly, because of this lack of a centralized project pattern and 
due to the age and nature of the existing uses within the Specific Plan area, the dilapidated character 
of these properties contribute to the overall blight affecting the surrounding area. Moreover, the 
existing uses at the site are disharmonious with the existing residential uses north of the plan area. 
The Commerce Retail Center Spec(/ic Plan. The objectives of the proposed project are to: 
• 
Develop a new retail center anchored by a regional-serving major anchor to provide the 
Trade Area with a centrally located, affordable, one-stop shopping alternative with an 
adequately sized grocery component to enhance customer convenience. 
• 
Positively contribute to the local economy via new capital investment, the creation of new 
employment opportunities, and the expansion of the City's tax base. 
• 
Provide a major anchor and complementary commercial uses that serves local residents and 
visitors with essential goods and services, in a safe and secure, 24-hour shopping 
environment. 
• Develop full-service retail uses near regional roadway and freeway facilities, and near other 
commercial uses to minimize travel lengths and utilize existing infrastructure to the 
maximum extent possible. 
• 
Implement the City of Commerce General Plan. 
• 
Implement a high-quality architectural design that improves the overall aesthetics of the 
project site and surrounding area. 
• Develop a project with new landscaping to soften the design and create a pleasant, attractive 
appearance. 


File No.A-16-110 
Page No. 3 
• Develop a site plan to minimize potential automobile and pedestrian conflicts. 
• 
Provide sufficient off-street parking to minimize impacts to the nearby residential 
neighborhood, and ensure that adequate onsite parking is provided for store customers, and 
employees. 
• Maximize economic growth and project in a way that serves the residents of the City of 
Commerce. 
On June 14, 2016, you provided the following additional facts: "Commerce is 63.8% industrial, 
19.3% circulation infrastructure, and 6.6% residential. Residential zones are lumped into pockets of 
the City, with approximately 3/4 of residential uses located off of Washington, Atlantic and Eastern." 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87 l 00 prohibits any state or local public official from making, participating in 
making, or using his or her official position to influence a government decision in which the official 
has an interest speci fied in Section 87103. A public official has a financial interest in a government 
decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a 
material financial effect on one or.more of the public official's interests, including an inter.est in real 
property. (Section 87103(b).) 
Foreseeability and Materiali(v 
Foreseeability: Regulation 18701(b), as applied to your facts, provides that a financial effect 
need not be likely to be considered reasonably foreseeable. In general, if the financial effect can be 
recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is reasonably 
foreseeable. If the financial result cannot be expected absent extraordinary circumstances not 
subject to the public official's control, it is not reasonably foreseeable.2 
Real Property Materiality: Many factors apply to determine whether a decision could have a 
material financial effect on real property. (See Regulation 18702.2.) As applies here, the financial 
effect will be material if the decision: 
• Would change the character of the parcel ofreal property by substantially altering traffic 
levels or intensity of use, including parking, of property surrounding the official's real 
property parcel, the view, privacy, noise levels, or air quality, including odors, or any other 
factors that would affect the market value of the real property parcel in which the official 
has a financial interest. (Regulation 18702(a)(10).) 
• Would consider any decision affecting real property value located within 500 feet of the 
property line of the official's real property, other than commercial property containing a 
business entity where the materiality standards are analyzed under Regulation 18702.1. 
1 There is a different test where an interest is explicitly in\'Olved in a decision, which does not apply here. 
(Regulation 1870l(a).) 


File No. A-16-110 
Page No. 4 
Notwithstanding this prohibition, the Commission may provide written advice allowing an 
official to participate under these circumstances if the Commission detennines that there are 
sufficient facts to indicate that there will be no reasonably foreseeable measurable impact on 
the official's property. (Regulation 18702(a)( 11 ).) 
• Would cause a reasonably prudent person, using due care and consideration under the 
circumstances, to believe that the governmental decision was of such a nature that its 
reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the market value of the official's property. 
(Regulation I 8702(a)(l 2).) 
Based on the magnitude of the project that is the subject of the governmental decision in 
relation to that property's current use, the council member would have a conflict of interest. This 
significant change the character of the parcel could substantially alter traffic levels and could affect 
the market value of the real property in the area. (Regulation 18702(a)(l 0) and ( 11 ).) Alternatively, 
the conversion of the commercial and industrial uses to retail could enhance the marketability of 
homes in the area. 
You noted that a financial impact on Councilmember Argumedo's domicile cannot be 
ascertained and is not "measurable" at this time. However, reasonable foreseeability does not 
require certainty or mathematical precision. In general, if the financial effect can be recognized as a 
realistic possibility (as tiere) and more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is reaűonably foreseeable. 
In this case a stated goal of the plan is to provide a major anchor and complementary commercial 
uses that serves local residents and visitors with essential goods and services, in a safe and secure, 
24-hour shopping environment. It is a realistic possibility that this goal will be achieved and it is a 
realistic possibility that this project will add value to the surrounding neighborhood. 
Thus, consistent with Regulation I 8702(a)( 12), the councilmember's participation in these 
decisions could reasonably lead to at least the perception that his decision-making could be 
compromised by financial advantages or disadvantages to his property. 
Public Ge11eral61 Exception. Commonly referred to as the "public generally" exception, 
Regulation I 8703(a) provides: 
"A governmental decision's financial effect on a public official's financial 
interest is indistinguishable from its effect on the public generally if the official 
establishes that a significant segment of the public is affected and the effect on his 
or her financial interest is not unique compared to the effect on the significant 
segment." 
For purposes of this provision, a "significant segment of the public" consists of at least 25 percent 
of ( l) the businesses or non-profit entities; (2) the real property, commercial real property, or 
residential real property; or (3) the individuals within the official's jurisdiction. (Regulation 
18703(b).) A "unique effect" on a public official's financial interest includes a disproportionate 
effect on: 
"( l) The development potential or use of the official• s real property or on 
the income producing potential of the official's real property or business entity. 


File No. A-16-110 
Page No. 5 
"(2) An official's business entity or real property resulting from the 
proximity of a project that is the subject of a decision. 
"(3) An official's interests in business entities or real properties resulting 
from the cumulative effect of the official's multiple interests in similar entities or 
properties that is substantially greater than the effect on a single interest. 
"(4) An official's interest in a business entity or real property resulting 
from the official's substantially !:,'Teater business volume or larger real property 
size when a decision affects all interests by the same or similar rate or percentage. 
"(5) A person's income, investments, assets or liabilities, or real property 
if the person is a source of income or gifts to the official. 
"(6) An official's personal finances or those of his or her immediate 
family." (Regulation l 8703(c).) 
You noted that Commerce is a small jurisdiction - approximately 12,500 residents and 6.5 square 
miles. It is also a unique City in that it mostly consists of industrial/manufacturing and commercial 
uses. Residential areas arc primarily limited to four pockets of the qty, with approximately¾ 
(75%) of the residential uses located off of Washington, Atlantic and Eastern. The councilmember's 
property is in the Washington/Atlantic pocket and separated from the project by other housing. The 
project area borders Washington and Atlantic on the opposite side of Washington. 
Considering the city's limited geographical size, the clustering of residential properties, and 
the significant size of the project, the Commerce Retail Center Specific Plan would appear to affect 
all the residential properties located off of Washington, Atlantic and Eastern (situated in a tight 
cluster near the site) in a similar way. Since this constitutes approximately¾ of residential uses in 
the city, the public generally exception would apply. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
JWW:jgl

location of the proposed project?

relation to that property's current use, the councilmember would have a conflict of interest. 
However, under the "public generally" exception the councilmember may participate in the 
decision.

Center Specific Plan despite the fact that his residence is located within 500 feet from the proposed 
location of the proposed project?

CONCLUSION: Based on the magnitude of the project that is the subject of the governmental decision in 
relation to that property's current use, the councilmember would have a conflict of interest. 
However, under the "public generally" exception the councilmember may participate in the 
decision.
